title: Logical dependency DAG for RiemannHypothesisViaWarpedStationarity.tex (single-point version)
description: Machine-checkable encoding of the proof culminating in Theorem thm:main (Riemann Hypothesis). Structure: the unwarping Y = D_{Theta^{-1}} Z is defined on R, time-average WSS with Bochner spectral measure mu_Y of total mass 2, band-limited to [-1,1] via a single IBP whose amplitude-over-phase-derivative ratio is rational in x = Theta'(t(u)) alone (no vartheta''), Paley-Wiener extends Y to an entire function of exponential type <= 1, Akhiezer-Laguerre-Polya places Y in the Laguerre-Polya class so every complex zero of Y is real. For each nontrivial zero rho = beta + i*gamma the single complex point t_rho = gamma + i(1/2 - beta) satisfies Z(t_rho) = 0 by the functional definition of Z and zeta(rho) = 0, and the pointwise identity Z = sqrt(Theta') * Y o Theta, extended along the vertical segment gamma + is by analytic continuation of its two sides, yields Y(Theta(t_rho)) = 0; reality of Y-zeros gives Theta(t_rho) in R, and the bound |Im Theta(a+ib)| >= |b| * min Re Theta' on the segment yields beta = 1/2.

AX-Digamma.name: Digamma series and positivity of Im psi'(1/4+it/2) for t>0
AX-Digamma.statement: psi'(s) = sum_{k>=0}(s+k)^{-2} for s not in {0,-1,-2,...}; at s = 1/4 + it/2, t>0, Im(s+k)^{-2} = -2(1/4+k)(t/2)/|s+k|^4 < 0 term by term, so Im psi'(1/4+it/2) < 0 and vartheta''(t) = -(1/4) Im psi'(1/4+it/2) > 0 on (0, infinity)

AX-Olver.name: Olver Stirling expansion for psi
AX-Olver.statement: psi(z) admits a Stirling expansion holomorphic in the sector |arg z| < pi - epsilon; applied at z = 1/4 + it/2 gives vartheta'(t) = (1/2) log(|t|/(2pi)) + O(|t|^{-1}) as |t| -> infinity in the open strip |Im t| < 1/2; in particular vartheta'(t) -> +infinity along R
AX-Olver.source: Olver, Asymptotics and Special Functions, Ch. 8 Sec. 4, Thm. 4.1

AX-Zdef.name: Z defined by its formula; holomorphic on the open strip
AX-Zdef.statement: Z(t) := e^{i vartheta(t)} zeta(1/2 + it); Z is real on R and holomorphic on {t in C : |Im t| < 1/2}, with vartheta holomorphic on this strip (poles of psi(1/4 + it/2) at Im t = +/- (2k+1/2), k >= 0) and zeta holomorphic on C \ {1}, so 1/2 + it avoids 1 for t not = -i/2 and -i/2 has |Im| = 1/2
AX-Zdef.source: Edwards, Riemann's Zeta Function, Sec. 6.5; Titchmarsh Ch. 2

AX-FE-zero.name: Zeros of zeta at rho = beta + i*gamma yield Z(t_rho) = 0
AX-FE-zero.statement: For a nontrivial zero rho = beta + i*gamma of zeta with 0 < beta < 1, the point t_rho := gamma + i(1/2 - beta) satisfies |Im t_rho| = |1/2 - beta| < 1/2, Z is holomorphic at t_rho by the formula Z(t) = e^{i vartheta(t)} zeta(1/2 + it), and Z(t_rho) = e^{i vartheta(t_rho)} zeta(1/2 + i t_rho) = e^{i vartheta(t_rho)} zeta(rho) = 0
AX-FE-zero.source: Direct substitution into the defining formula of Z

AX-FE-symmetry.name: Functional-equation symmetry of nontrivial zeros
AX-FE-symmetry.statement: If rho = beta + i*gamma is a nontrivial zero of zeta, so is 1 - conj(rho) = (1 - beta) + i*gamma; hence proving Re(rho) = 1/2 for beta in (0, 1/2] suffices
AX-FE-symmetry.source: Titchmarsh Ch. 2 Sec. 2.12 (functional equation xi(s) = xi(1-s))

AX-HL.name: Hardy-Littlewood second moment
AX-HL.statement: integral_0^T |zeta(1/2 + it)|^2 dt = T log(T/(2 pi)) + (2 gamma - 1) T + O(T^{1/2} log T)
AX-HL.source: Titchmarsh, Theory of the Riemann Zeta-Function, Ch. VII Thm. 7.3; Hardy-Littlewood, Acta Math. 41 (1918)

AX-RS.name: Riemann-Siegel formula with phase-derivative remainder bound
AX-RS.statement: Z(t) = 2 sum_{n <= N(t)} n^{-1/2} cos(vartheta(t) - t log n) + R(t) with N(t) = floor(sqrt(t/(2 pi))), R(t) = O(t^{-1/4}), and (d/dt) arg R(t) = O(t^{-1/2}); the n-th mode has stationary point at t = 2 pi n^2 where vartheta'(t) = log n
AX-RS.source: Edwards, Riemann's Zeta Function, Sec. 7.4 Thm. 7.1; Gabcke Dissertation Goettingen 1979 Satz 7.1; Berry, Proc. Roy. Soc. A 450 (1995) eq. 18

AX-Bochner.name: Bochner theorem for positive-definite continuous functions
AX-Bochner.statement: A continuous positive-definite function on R is the Fourier transform of a finite non-negative Borel measure on R

AX-WK.name: Wiener Theorem 29 (spectral measure via windowed Fourier transform)
AX-WK.statement: For time-average WSS f with covariance c_f the Fourier transform of mu_f, and K_T(mu) := (2 pi)^{-1} integral_0^T f(u) e^{-i mu u} du, for bounded Borel E in R: mu_f(E) = lim_{T->inf} (2 pi / T) integral_E |K_T(mu)|^2 d mu

AX-PW-WSS.name: Paley-Wiener for time-average WSS functions with compactly supported spectral measure
AX-PW-WSS.statement: If f : R -> C is time-average WSS with covariance c_f(eta) = integral e^{i eta xi} d mu_f(xi) and supp mu_f is contained in [-sigma, sigma], then f extends to an entire function on C of exponential type at most sigma with |f(z)| <= mu_f(R)^{1/2} e^{sigma |Im z|}
AX-PW-WSS.source: Yaglom, Correlation Theory of Stationary and Related Random Functions, Vol. I, Thm. 4.7.3; Bochner-Phillips, Ann. Math. 43 (1942), 409-418

AX-Akhiezer.name: Akhiezer-Levin Laguerre-Polya criterion
AX-Akhiezer.statement: A real-valued entire function F of exponential type at most tau whose autocorrelation measure is non-negative, finite, and supported in [-2 tau, 2 tau] belongs to the Laguerre-Polya class; every complex zero of F is real
AX-Akhiezer.source: Akhiezer, Theory of Approximation, Sec. V.4; Levin, Lectures on Entire Functions, Lecture 16 Thm. 3

lem:cstar.name: c_* := -inf vartheta' on R is finite
lem:cstar.statement: vartheta' is continuous on R and vartheta'(t) -> +infinity as |t| -> infinity (AX-Olver), so c_* := -inf_{t in R} vartheta'(t) is finite

def:Theta.name: Shifted phase Theta(t) = vartheta(t) + c t with c > c_*
def:Theta.statement: For c > c_*, set Theta(t) := vartheta(t) + c t; then vartheta' + c > 0 on R

lem:Theta.name: Properties of Theta on the real line
lem:Theta.statement: For c > c_*, Theta = vartheta + c * id : R -> R is a C^infty bijection with Theta' = vartheta' + c > 0 on R and C^infty inverse.

def:DOT.name: Omer-Torresani warping operator
def:DOT.statement: For a C^1 diffeomorphism gamma : R -> R with gamma' > 0, [D_gamma x](u) := sqrt(gamma'(u)) x(gamma(u))

lem:unitary.name: Unitarity of D_gamma on L^2(R)
lem:unitary.statement: D_gamma : L^2(R) -> L^2(R) is unitary with D_gamma^{-1} = D_{gamma^{-1}}
lem:unitary.role: auxiliary: records the L^2 unitarity of the warping operator for expository context; not invoked in the single-point proof chain to thm:main, which uses only the pointwise factorization identity lem:Zfact

def:Y.name: Unwarped function Y : R -> R
def:Y.statement: Y(u) := Z(Theta^{-1}(u))/sqrt(Theta'(Theta^{-1}(u))) = [D_{Theta^{-1}} Z](u) on R; Y is real since Z is real on R and Theta' > 0 on R

lem:Zfact.name: Pointwise factorization Z(t) = sqrt(Theta'(t)) Y(Theta(t)) on R
lem:Zfact.statement: For every t in R, Z(t) = sqrt(Theta'(t)) * Y(Theta(t)); an immediate consequence of the definition Y(u) = Z(Theta^{-1}(u))/sqrt(Theta'(Theta^{-1}(u)))

def:covg.name: Time-average covariance
def:covg.statement: c_f(eta) := lim_{U->inf} U^{-1} integral_0^U f(u) conj(f(u+eta)) du when the limit exists

lem:HL.name: Hardy-Littlewood second moment (paper statement)
lem:HL.statement: integral_0^T |zeta(1/2 + it)|^2 dt = T log(T/(2 pi)) + (2 gamma - 1) T + O(T^{1/2} log T)

lem:RS.name: Riemann-Siegel expansion (paper statement)
lem:RS.statement: Z(t) = 2 sum_{n <= N(t)} n^{-1/2} cos(vartheta(t) - t log n) + R(t), N(t) = floor(sqrt(t/(2 pi))), R(t) = O(t^{-1/4}), (d/dt) arg R(t) = O(t^{-1/2})

lem:omegabound.name: Instantaneous u-frequency ratio
lem:omegabound.statement: For every n >= 1 and t >= 2 pi n^2, omega_n(t) := (vartheta'(t) - log n)/(vartheta'(t) + c) lies in [0, 1). Non-negativity: vartheta'' > 0 on (0, infinity) (AX-Digamma) and vartheta'(2 pi n^2) = log n + O(n^{-2}) (AX-Olver at t = 2 pi n^2) give vartheta'(t) >= log n for t >= 2 pi n^2. Strict upper bound: (vartheta'(t) + c) - (vartheta'(t) - log n) = c + log n > 0, so omega_n(t) = 1 - (c + log n)/(vartheta'(t) + c) < 1

thm:Ywss.name: Time-average WSS of Y
thm:Ywss.statement: c_Y(eta) exists for every eta in R and depends only on eta; c_Y(0) = 2. Proof: U^{-1} integral_0^U |Y|^2 du = U^{-1} integral_0^{Theta^{-1}(U)} |Z|^2 dt -> 2 via lem:HL and Theta(T) ~ T/2 log T. Product split via lem:RS: diagonal (n,n,sigma,sigma) gives A(eta) by bounded convergence against (omega_n)_* Leb with omega_n in [0,1) by lem:omegabound; off-diagonal and antidiagonal and remainder cross-terms are o(U)

cor:Cramer.name: Bochner spectral measure of Y
cor:Cramer.statement: There is a finite non-negative Borel measure mu_Y on R with c_Y(eta) = integral e^{i eta xi} d mu_Y(xi) and mu_Y(R) = 2

def:KT.name: Windowed Fourier transform and power spectral density
def:KT.statement: K_T(mu) := (2 pi)^{-1} integral_0^T Z(t) sqrt(Theta'(t)) e^{-i mu Theta(t)} dt; S(mu) := limsup_{T->inf} 2 pi |K_T(mu)|^2 / Theta(T)

thm:bandlim.name: Band-limit theorem via single integration by parts
thm:bandlim.statement: For every c > c_* and every |mu| > 1: S(mu) = 0. Proof: insert lem:RS into K_T and substitute u = Theta(t). Mode (n, sigma) contributes J_{n,sigma}(T,mu) = integral G_{n,sigma}(u) e^{i Psi} du with G_{n,sigma}(u) = Theta'(t(u))^{-1/2} = x^{-1/2}, x = Theta'(t(u)), and Psi'(u) = sigma omega_n(t(u)) - mu, so |Psi'| >= |mu| - 1 > 0. The IBP ratio Q = x^{1/2}/((sigma - mu) x - sigma (c + log n)) is a rational function of x alone (no vartheta'' enters, and none is required); |dQ/dx| = O(x^{-3/2}). Single IBP yields |J_{n,sigma}| <= C/(lambda (log n)^{1/2}), summation gives |K_T(mu)| = O(T^{1/4}/sqrt(log T)), and 2 pi |K_T|^2 / Theta(T) -> 0

cor:supp.name: Support of mu_Y is contained in [-1, 1]

thm:PW.name: Paley-Wiener entire extension of Y
thm:PW.statement: Y extends to an entire function of exponential type at most 1 with |Y(z)| <= sqrt(2) e^{|Im z|} on C

thm:Akhiezer.name: Akhiezer-Laguerre-Polya criterion (paper statement)
thm:Akhiezer.statement: A real-valued entire function F of exponential type at most tau whose time-average autocorrelation measure is finite, non-negative, and supported in [-2 tau, 2 tau] lies in the Laguerre-Polya class and has every complex zero real

thm:Yzeros.name: Reality of zeros of Y
thm:Yzeros.statement: Every complex zero of the entire extension of Y is real

thm:main.name: Riemann Hypothesis (segment-only 1D identity theorem)
thm:main.statement: Every nontrivial zero rho = beta + i gamma of zeta satisfies beta = 1/2. Proof: by AX-FE-symmetry suffices to treat beta in (0, 1/2]. Z is holomorphic on Omega := {|Im t| < 1/2} (vartheta holomorphic there by AX-Digamma; zeta(1/2 + it) holomorphic on Omega since its only pole at 1/2 + it = 1 corresponds to t = -i/2 on the boundary). Let t(s) := gamma + i s for s in C; then t(ir) = gamma - r in R for real r, t(1/2 - beta) = t_rho := gamma + i(1/2 - beta). Let m_rho := -inf_{s in [0, 1/2 - beta]} Re vartheta'(gamma + i s), finite by continuity of vartheta' on the compact segment inside Omega. Fix c > max(c_*, m_rho). Let N := t^{-1}(Omega) = {|Re s| < 1/2}, open connected, containing [0, 1/2 - beta] and iR. Define F(s) := Z(t(s)) and G(s) := sqrt(Theta'(t(s))) * Y(Theta(t(s))) on N (principal branch of sqrt). Let N' be the connected component of {s in N : Theta'(t(s)) not in (-infty, 0]} containing [0, 1/2 - beta]; F and G are holomorphic on N' (Z holomorphic on Omega; Y entire by thm:PW); N' contains an imaginary interval {ir : |r| < epsilon} since 0 is in N' and N' is open. For s = ir in N', t(ir) = gamma - r is real and lem:Zfact at gamma - r gives F(ir) = Z(gamma - r) = sqrt(Theta'(gamma - r)) Y(Theta(gamma - r)) = G(ir). By the 1D identity theorem on the connected open N' subset of C, F = G on N'. Evaluating at s_0 = 1/2 - beta: Z(t_rho) = sqrt(Theta'(t_rho)) Y(Theta(t_rho)). Since 1/2 + i t_rho = rho, AX-FE-zero gives Z(t_rho) = 0; sqrt(Theta'(t_rho)) != 0 since Theta'(t_rho) not in (-infty, 0]; hence Y(Theta(t_rho)) = 0. By thm:Yzeros, Theta(t_rho) in R. Compute Im Theta(t_rho) = Im Theta(gamma) + integral_0^{1/2 - beta} Re Theta'(gamma + i s) ds = integral_0^{1/2 - beta} Re Theta'(gamma + i s) ds (since Theta(gamma) in R) >= (1/2 - beta) (c - m_rho). Left side is 0; c - m_rho > 0; therefore 1/2 - beta = 0, so beta = 1/2.

notes: Single-point version: no parametric sub-strip Omega_delta, no holomorphic bijection framework, no identity theorem applied to the factorization on a 2D domain, no strip-wide zero-set bijection, no strip-wide reality corollary.

Use of complex values is confined to: (1) holomorphy of vartheta' and zeta in a neighborhood of the single point t_rho = gamma + i(1/2 - beta) (via AX-Zdef, lem:Theta (ii)); (2) analytic continuation along the vertical segment gamma + is, s in [0, 1/2 - beta], of the identity Z(t) = sqrt(Theta'(t)) Y(Theta(t)) whose two sides are holomorphic there and agree at s = 0; (3) the elementary identity Im Theta(a+ib) = integral_0^b Re Theta'(a+is) ds for the imaginary-part bound.

Primary chain: AX-Olver -> lem:cstar -> def:Theta; def:Theta + AX-Olver + AX-Digamma + AX-Zdef -> lem:Theta; def:DOT + lem:Theta -> def:Y -> lem:Zfact; def:covg + lem:HL + lem:RS + lem:omegabound + lem:Theta -> thm:Ywss -> cor:Cramer; def:KT + lem:RS + lem:omegabound + lem:Theta -> thm:bandlim -> cor:supp; thm:Ywss + cor:Cramer + cor:supp -> thm:PW; thm:PW + thm:Akhiezer + cor:supp + def:Y -> thm:Yzeros; AX-FE-symmetry + AX-FE-zero + AX-Zdef + def:Theta + lem:Theta + lem:cstar + lem:Zfact + thm:PW + thm:Yzeros -> thm:main.
Axioms: AX-Digamma, AX-Olver, AX-Zdef, AX-FE-zero, AX-FE-symmetry, AX-HL, AX-RS, AX-Bochner, AX-WK, AX-PW-WSS, AX-Akhiezer.
